reasoning: Each informal hypothesis maps precisely to the Lean version: continuity on [0,∞) via ContinuousOn on Set.Ici 0; differentiability on (0,∞) via DifferentiableOn on Set.Ioi 0; f(0)=0 directly; and f' monotone increasing via MonotoneOn (deriv f) on Set.Ioi 0. Since f is differentiable on Set.Ioi 0, `deriv f` agrees with the actual derivative there, so using `deriv f` is safe. Rudin defines "monotonically increasing" as non-decreasing (Def. 4.28), matching Mathlib's MonotoneOn. The conclusion restricts g = f x / x to Set.Ioi 0, matching the informal "g is monotonically increasing for x > 0". No hidden hypotheses are added or dropped.
